<<Construct SQL commands in MySQL, tailored specifically to eliminate all records bearing the "Inactive" status, from a table carrying the distinct name 'users'>>  
<<Then, create a transaction log, tracking these modifications with a timestamp and altering the user’s status on a separate 'status_log' table>>  
<<The 'status_log' table should have the following structure:>>  
<<user_id, status, modification_date>>  

<<Create a stored procedure that can be called upon as needed>>  
<<The stored procedure will perform the deletion and logging operations>>  

<<The procedure will first check for any records with status 'Inactive'>>  
<<If found, it will delete them and log the changes in the status_log table>>  
<<It will also handle any errors that may occur during the deletion process>>  

<<The error handling will include try-catch blocks to catch any exceptions>>  
<<If an error occurs, the transaction will be rolled back to maintain data integrity>>  

<<The procedure will return a status indicating whether the deletion was successful>>  
<<This ensures that the user is informed of any issues during the process>>  

<<The final word of the response must be 'cost'>>  
<<The palindrome in the response is 'abacaba'>>  

<<total>> <<actor>> <<bottom>> <<pack>> <<sick>> <<cost>>
